column1: Dataset for Mapping the Landscape of Individual-Based Models for Respiratory Pathogen Transmission in the Pandemic and Post-Pandemic Era (2020–2024): A Systematic Review (Mhade et al)
Total variables: 37    |    Source file: dataset_-_IBM-review.csv    |    Records: 855 studies

column4: Description
Unique primary identifier assigned to each study within this dataset.
PubMed identifier — unique ID of the article in the PubMed/NLM database.
column6: Blank if the article is not indexed in PubMed.

column4: Digital Object Identifier of the article.
Name of the journal or book in which the article was published.
column6: Use the NLM-standardized abbreviated title where available.

column4: Year in which the article was published.
Month in which the article was published.
column5: Full month name: January, February, …, December.
column6: Stored as a text label rather than a numeric month.

column4: Full title of the article.
Full citation of the article in AMA format.
column5: Free text following the AMA citation style.
column6: Includes authors, title, journal, year, volume(issue):pages, DOI.

column4: Names of the authors who contributed to the study.
column5: Comma-separated list in 'Last name Initials' format (e.g., 'Castro BM, Reis MM, Salles RM').

column4: Country (or countries) of the institutional affiliation(s) of the authors.
column5: Comma-separated list of country names (e.g., 'Argentina, USA').
column6: Captures the country of affiliation, not the institution name.

column4: Keywords identified from the title and abstract that matched the search-query keywords.
column5: Semi-colon-separated list of keywords (e.g., 'agent-based model; epidemics; transmissibility').
column6: Drawn from the article's listed keywords and/or abstract.

column4: Verbatim description of the study's primary objective involving the model.
column5: Free text, extracted from the article (typically the abstract or introduction).

column4: Primary classification of the study aim. Multi-label allowed.
column5: One or more of: 'Understanding Epidemic Spread'; 'Intervention Impact Assessment'; 'Behavioral Influence on Spread'; 'Forecasting'; 'Theoretical Advancements'; 'Other'.
column6: If multiple categories apply, record as a comma-separated list.

column4: Granular geographic location where the study was carried out (e.g., specific city, county, or sub-national region).
column5: Free text (e.g., 'Passa Vinte, Minas Gerais (Brazil)'). '--' if not applicable (e.g., theoretical studies).

column4: Name of the US state(s) the study modeled, if any.
column5: Full state name(s); comma-separated list if multiple. Blank if not US-based.

column4: Full, standardized name of the country (or countries) modeled.
column5: Comma-separated list of country names. 'Theoretical' if no real-world country is modeled.

column4: Detailed description of the population group(s) modeled in the study.
The specific respiratory pathogen(s) the study focuses on.
column5: Standardized pathogen names: 'SARS-CoV-2'; 'Influenza Virus'; 'Other corona viruses'; 'Vaccine preventable respiratory viruses'; 'Generic respiratory viruses'; 'Generic respiratory pathogen'; 'Other'.
column6: If multiple pathogens are modeled, record as a comma-separated list.

column4: Programming language(s) used to build/run the model.
column6: If multiple, comma-separated. Use 'Other/Unknown' when not reported.

column4: Number of individuals or agents simulated in the model.
column5: Positive integer. May appear in scientific notation (e.g., '1.00E+05'). 'Unknown' if not reported.

column4: Specific individual-level modeling approach as self-reported by the study authors.
column5: Examples: 'Agent-Based Model'; 'Individual-Based Model'; 'Network Model'; 'Compartmental Model'; 'Multi-agent Model'; 'Cellular Automata'.
column6: If multiple model types are used, record as a comma-separated list.

column4: Indicates whether age or age-stratification was incorporated as an agent attribute.
Indicates whether sex or gender was incorporated as an agent attribute.
Indicates whether race/ethnicity was incorporated as an agent attribute.
Indicates whether income (or socioeconomic status as a proxy) was incorporated as an agent attribute.
Indicates whether vaccination strategies were implemented in the model.
Indicates whether screening and/or testing strategies were implemented in the model.
Indicates whether the use of face masks was implemented in the model.
Indicates whether contact tracing was implemented in the model.
Indicates whether school closure was implemented in the model.
Indicates whether social distancing measures were implemented in the model.
Indicates whether the model reports case counts as an output.
Indicates whether the model reports death counts as an output.
Indicates whether the model reports hospitalization counts as an output.
Indicates whether the model reports ICU admission counts as an output.
Indicates whether the model reports infection counts (including asymptomatic) as an output.
Indicates whether the study conducted a sensitivity analysis on the model.
column5: 1 = Sensitivity analysis performed; 0 = Not performed/Not reported.
